judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and comparable on expectations a–c, but Report 2 answered the task better overall because it correctly recommends dropping the hardcoded webhook count (d) where Report 1 instead perpetuates it by just updating the number to 5.

report_1.evidence: New refund provider calls should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — constructs its own `chargekit.Client(...)` and calls `client.charge(...)` directly. `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9` — constructs its own `chargekit.Client(...)` and calls `.charge(...)` directly. (`backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` also imports from `chargekit`, but it only re-exports exception classes and is the explicitly documented allowed exception, so I do not count it as a violation.)

report_2.evidence: New provider calls should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — constructs its own `chargekit.Client(...)` and calls `.charge(...)` directly. `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13`... The only legitimate, documented exception is `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`, which imports *only* the SDK's error types

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:15-28`) has a **6th** field, `external_ref`, which `from_model` always populates. `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` confirms this column was added.

report_2.evidence: the actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-28`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which `from_model` populates and `invoices.py:17-18` sets from the charge result. This field is backed by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`.

report_1.evidence: Correct that line now, independently of the refund feature, so the doc stops overstating "exactly 5 fields."... update the doc **in the same change that changes the corresponding behavior**, so code and doc move together and the doc isn't left stale between commits.

report_2.evidence: this should be surfaced and resolved now, before/at the start of the refund work, not deferred... the doc must be updated in the **same commit** as the code it governs and called out in that commit/PR summary
this is exactly the "don't cite a number, read the source" case — a hard-coded count that has already drifted. I'd drop the "4" and instead point at `webhooks.py` as the source of truth
